title: Peer-Relative Pace Pressure Features
summary: Official F1 undercut analysis emphasizes that strategy hinges on how fast fresh tyres are relative to rivals on used tyres, so pit calls should depend on losing pace versus same-lap peers, not only on absolute tyre age or raw lap time.
rationale: Official F1 undercut analysis emphasizes that strategy hinges on how fast fresh tyres are relative to rivals on used tyres, so pit calls should depend on losing pace versus same-lap peers, not only on absolute tyre age or raw lap time. The current best code only uses coarse medians by race or compound, not contemporaneous cross-sectional pressure.
implementation_hint: For each `Race_Year x LapNumber x Compound`, compute percentile/rank of `LapTime_Delta`, gap to same-compound median and fastest lap, and change versus the previous lap’s peer rank. Add position-bin versions to proxy dirty-air pressure and combine with `TyreLife` or `CurrentTyreFinishExcess`.
expected_effect: Improves detection of undercut-vulnerable laps and track-specific wear windows where a driver suddenly becomes slow relative to the active compound cohort.
risk: Cross-sectional features are noisy under traffic, blue flags, and mixed SC/VSC states; wet-tyre groups are sparse and need shrinkage.
